revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:愛宕社の火祭り]]



'''Atago-sha no Hi Matsuri''' (愛宕社の火祭り) is a {{ill|festival|ja|祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q132241}} held annually on {{ill|January 26|en|January 26|qid=Q2282|lt=January 26|1=January 26}} in {{ill|Uozu, Toyama|en|Uozu, Toyama|qid=Q819613|lt=Uozu, Toyama|1=Uozu, Toyama}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|Toyama Prefecture|qid=Q132929|lt=Toyama Prefecture|1=Toyama Prefecture}}. The festival involves burning {{ill|Gohei|en|Gohei|qid=Q1011372|lt=Gohei|1=Gohei}}, which resemble firemen’s matoi banners, to pray for fire safety, household security, and good health for the year. In the days leading up to the festival, each district prepares and erects its own gohei as part of the traditional custom. In {{ill|2007|en|2007|qid=Q2024|lt=2007|1=2007}} ({{ill|2007|en|2007|qid=Q2024|lt=Heisei 19|1=2007}}), it was selected as part of the *100 Cultural Properties of Toyama* (*Toyama no Bunkazai Hyakusen*), specifically under the *Annual Events of Toyama* category.
Although the festival is named after *Atago-sha* (愛宕社), it is actually held at {{ill|Uozu Shrine|ja|魚津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu Shrine|qq=}}. This is because the original Atago Shrine, formerly located within the grounds of {{ill|Uozu City Omachi Elementary School|ja|魚津市立大町小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu City Omachi Elementary School|1=Uozu City Omachi Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11674028}}, was merged with Uozu Shrine in {{ill|1966|en|1966|qid=Q2649|lt=1966|1=1966}} ({{ill|1966|en|1966|qid=Q2649|lt=Showa 41|1=1966}}).

== See Also ==
* {{ill|Uozu Shrine|ja|魚津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu Shrine|qq=}}
* {{ill|List of Festivals of Japan|ja|日本の祭り一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Festivals of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の祭一覧|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の祭一覧|1=List of festivals in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10312376}}
== References ==
* *Toyama no Bunkazai Hyakusen Series (4) Toyama no Nenchū Gyōji* (富山の文化財百選シリーズ(4) とやまの年中行事), Toyama Prefecture {{ill|Board of education|en|Board of education|qid=Q399541|lt=Board of Education|1=Board of education}}, Lifelong Learning and Cultural Properties Division, published in {{ill|2008|en|2008|qid=Q2004|lt=2008|1=2008}} ({{ill|2008|en|2008|qid=Q2004|lt=Heisei 20|1=2008}}).
== External Links ==
* {{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.uozu-kanko.jp/?p=1192 |title=愛宕社の火祭り／魚津市観光協会公式サイト 魚津たびナビ |accessdate=2015-04-22 |qq=}}
* [http://www.pref.toyama.jp/sections/3009/3007/digital/03-event/gyouji/gyouji.pdf *Toyama no Bunkazai Hyakusen Series (4) Toyama no Nenchū Gyōji* (富山の文化財百選シリーズ(4) とやまの年中行事), Toyama Prefecture Board of Education, Lifelong Learning and Cultural Properties Division]

{{Atago Faith|qq=}}

{{translated page|ja|愛宕社の火祭り|version=98068912|comment=Imported full ja history}}



[[ja:愛宕社の火祭り]]

[[ja:v]]

<!--Atago Fire Festival--><!--Atago Fire Festival--><!--Atago Fire Festival--><!--Atago Fire Festival--><!--Atago Fire Festival--><!--Atago Fire Festival-->

[[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]
==Merged second translation==
'''Atagosha no Himatsuri''' (愛宕社の火祭り) is a {{ill|festival|ja|祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q132241}} held annually on {{ill|January 26|en|January 26|qid=Q2282|lt=January 26|1=January 26}} in {{ill|Uozu, Toyama|en|Uozu, Toyama|qid=Q819613|lt=Uozu, Toyama|1=Uozu, Toyama}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|Toyama Prefecture|qid=Q132929|lt=Toyama Prefecture|1=Toyama Prefecture}}. During the festival, {{ill|Gohei|en|Gohei|qid=Q1011372|lt=gohei|1=Gohei}}, resembling firefighting banners, are burned to pray for fire prevention, household safety, and good health for the coming year. In the days leading up to the event, locals from different districts craft gohei for the festival and display them in the area. In {{ill|2007|en|2007|qid=Q2024|lt=2007|1=2007}} ({{ill|2007|en|2007|qid=Q2024|lt=Heisei 19|1=2007}}), the festival was selected as part of the "100 Cultural Assets of Toyama" (Annual Events Category).
Although the festival is called "Atagosha" after the shrine, it is actually held at {{ill|Uozu Shrine|ja|魚津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu Shrine|qq=}}. This is because the original Atagosha Shrine, which was located on the grounds of {{ill|Uozu Municipal Omachi Elementary School|ja|魚津市立大町小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu Municipal Omachi Elementary School|1=Uozu City Omachi Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11674028}}, merged with Uozu Shrine in {{ill|1966|en|1966|qid=Q2649|lt=1966|1=1966}} (Showa 41).

== See Also ==
* {{ill|Uozu Shrine|ja|魚津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uozu Shrine|qq=}}
* {{ill|List of festivals in Japan|ja|日本の祭り一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of festivals in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の祭一覧|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の祭一覧|1=List of festivals in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10312376}}
== References ==
* 『とやまの文化財百選シリーズ(4) とやまの年中行事』, Toyama Prefecture Board of Education, Lifelong Learning and Cultural Heritage Division, {{ill|2008|en|2008|qid=Q2004|lt=2008|1=2008}} (Heisei 20), March edition.
== External Links ==
* {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.uozu-kanko.jp/?p=1192 |title=愛宕社の火祭り／魚津市観光協会公式サイト 魚津たびナビ |accessdate=2015-04-22 |qq=}}
* [http://www.pref.toyama.jp/sections/3009/3007/digital/03-event/gyouji/gyouji.pdf 『とやまの文化財百選シリーズ(4) とやまの年中行事』 (Toyama Prefecture Board of Education, Lifelong Learning and Cultural Heritage Division)]
{{Event-stub|qq=}}
{{Atago Faith|qq=}}
